description: A formal, high-level document that authorizes the project, defines its objectives, identifies key stakeholders, and outlines the project manager's authority. It serves as the foundation for all subsequent project planning and execution activities. Includes project goals, scope, high-level risks, and budget overview.
steps_to_create: Define project goals and objectives based on the project plan.
Identify key stakeholders and their roles.
Outline the project scope, deliverables, and high-level milestones.
Define the project's high-level budget and resource requirements.
Identify initial risks and assumptions.
Obtain approval from key stakeholders.

description: A high-level framework outlining the approach to integrating new and existing technologies into the game's development. It defines the criteria for selecting technologies, the process for evaluating their feasibility, and the plan for integrating them into the game's core systems. It addresses the balance between innovation and stability, aligning with the chosen 'Builder's Foundation' scenario.
steps_to_create: Define the project's technological goals and objectives.
Identify potential new technologies to integrate.
Evaluate the feasibility and risks associated with each technology.
Develop a plan for integrating selected technologies into the game's core systems.
Define success metrics for technological integration.
Obtain approval from key technical stakeholders.

description: A high-level framework outlining the approach to designing the game's open world. It defines the scope, size, and characteristics of the world, as well as the process for creating and populating it with content. It addresses the balance between scope and performance, aligning with the chosen 'Builder's Foundation' scenario.
steps_to_create: Define the scope and size of the open world.
Determine the characteristics of the world (e.g., urban, rural, diverse landscapes).
Develop a plan for creating and populating the world with content.
Define success metrics for world design.
Obtain approval from key design stakeholders.

description: A high-level plan outlining the approach to using procedural content generation in the game's development. It defines the types of content to be generated procedurally, the tools and techniques to be used, and the process for integrating procedural content with handcrafted content. It addresses the balance between cost and quality, aligning with the chosen 'Builder's Foundation' scenario. Includes specific mitigation strategies for AI bias.
steps_to_create: Define the types of content to be generated procedurally.
Select the tools and techniques to be used for procedural generation.
Develop a plan for integrating procedural content with handcrafted content.
Define success metrics for procedural content generation.
Outline strategies for mitigating AI bias in generated content.
Obtain approval from key technical and design stakeholders.

description: A high-level plan outlining the approach to securing funding for the game's development. It defines the sources of funding to be pursued, the process for securing funding, and the plan for managing relationships with funding partners. It addresses the balance between creative control and financial stability, aligning with the chosen 'Builder's Foundation' scenario.
steps_to_create: Identify potential sources of funding (e.g., publisher investment, strategic partnerships, government grants).
Develop a plan for securing funding from each source.
Define the terms and conditions of funding agreements.
Develop a plan for managing relationships with funding partners.
Define success metrics for funding diversification.
Obtain approval from key financial stakeholders.

description: A high-level framework outlining the approach to defining the ethical framework within the game world. It defines the types of moral choices to be offered to players, the consequences of those choices, and the overall tone and themes of the game. It addresses the balance between player comfort and social commentary, aligning with the chosen 'Builder's Foundation' scenario.
steps_to_create: Define the types of moral choices to be offered to players.
Determine the consequences of those choices.
Define the overall tone and themes of the game.
Develop a plan for implementing the morality system in the game.
Define success metrics for the moral ambiguity spectrum.
Obtain approval from key narrative and design stakeholders.

description: A comprehensive document that identifies potential risks to the project, assesses their likelihood and impact, and outlines mitigation strategies. It serves as a central repository for all risk-related information and is regularly updated throughout the project lifecycle. Includes technical, financial, social, and operational risks.
steps_to_create: Identify potential risks based on the project plan and assumptions.
Assess the likelihood and impact of each risk.
Develop mitigation strategies for each risk.
Assign ownership of each risk and mitigation strategy.
Regularly update the risk register throughout the project lifecycle.

description: A document that outlines how project information will be communicated to stakeholders. It defines the communication channels, frequency, and content for different stakeholder groups. It ensures that all stakeholders are kept informed of project progress, risks, and issues.
steps_to_create: Identify key stakeholders and their communication needs.
Define the communication channels to be used.
Determine the frequency and content of communications.
Assign responsibility for each communication activity.
Obtain approval from key stakeholders.

description: A document that outlines how stakeholders will be engaged throughout the project lifecycle. It defines the strategies for building relationships with stakeholders, gathering their feedback, and managing their expectations. It ensures that stakeholders are actively involved in the project and their concerns are addressed.
steps_to_create: Identify key stakeholders and their interests.
Define the strategies for engaging with each stakeholder group.
Determine the frequency and methods for gathering stakeholder feedback.
Develop a plan for managing stakeholder expectations.
Obtain approval from key stakeholders.
